signals.signal_summary: Blinkit achieved first-ever adjusted EBITDA positive at Rs 4 crore in Q3 FY26, vs Rs 103 crore loss a year earlier. Revenue Rs 12,256 crore (186% YoY) due to inventory model shift.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Log as milestone event; update EBITDA trajectory to profitable
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Revenue inflated Rs 776% YoY due to inventory model shift — NOV is correct comparable, not revenue

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit adjusted EBITDA jumped 9x QoQ to Rs 37 crore (0.3% of NOV) in Q4 FY26, up from Rs 4 crore in Q3 FY26 and a loss of Rs 178 crore in Q4 FY25.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update EBITDA margin tracker; flag thin 0.3% NOV margin as fragile
signals.limitation_or_caveat: 0.3% NOV margin is razor-thin; competitive intensity may compress further

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit NOV grew 95.4% YoY to Rs 14,386 crore in Q4 FY26; revenue up 186% YoY (inventory model shift). Net AOV flat at Rs 525. Added 216 net new stores to reach 2,243 total.
signals.source_name: Eternal Q4 FY26 Filing / Daily Hunt
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update NOV, AOV, and store count panels; compare with Instamart AOV Rs 700
signals.limitation_or_caveat: AOV flat YoY (Rs 520->525) suggests limited pricing power or category mix shift

signals.signal_summary: Eternal consolidated net profit surged 346% YoY to Rs 174 crore; revenue Rs 17,292 crore; consolidated adjusted EBITDA Rs 429 crore. Targeting $1B EBITDA by FY29.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update Eternal consolidated P&L panel; track FY29 $1B EBITDA target
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Revenue jump largely driven by Blinkit inventory model accounting change, not purely organic growth

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit FY25 adjusted EBITDA loss Rs 292 crore, significantly narrower than Instamart's Rs 2,095 crore adjusted EBITDA loss in the same period.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add to FY25 comparative panel; track YoY improvement to FY26 EBITDA+
signals.limitation_or_caveat: FY25 metric; transitioned to inventory model in Q1 FY26 making YoY comparison complex

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit has >50% quick-commerce market share as of Sep 2025, up from ~46% in late 2024.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update market share panel; Blinkit dominant benchmark
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Based on GMV share; quarterly fluctuations possible

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit generated over Rs 400 crore in ad revenue in a single quarter, crossing Rs 1,000 crore annualised. Ad revenue now constitutes ~15% of total revenue.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add retail media revenue tracker; benchmark QC ad revenue as % of total
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Annualised figure extrapolated from quarterly run rate; not audited fiscal-year number

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit ended Q4 FY26 with 2,243 dark stores, adding 216 net new stores. Targeting 3,000 stores by March 2027. 5-6% EBITDA margin guided.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update dark store count; track toward 3,000 target
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Store count includes newly launched stores that may not be at full contribution margin yet

signals.signal_summary: Univest analyst note flagged Blinkit EBITDA margin at -3% vs guided breakeven, with NOV missing estimates. Contradicts official Rs 37 crore positive EBITDA figure.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Flag discrepancy between reported adj. EBITDA Rs 37 Cr (0.3% NOV) and analyst -3% estimate; likely uses different denominator
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Conflicts with official reported EBITDA+ figure; likely uses GOV vs NOV as denominator

signals.signal_summary: Instamart GOV grew 68.8% YoY to Rs 7,881 crore but saw first-ever sequential decline of -0.7% vs Rs 7,938 crore in Q3 FY26.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Flag first QoQ decline as potential inflection point; track Q1 FY27 data
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Sequential decline may be seasonal; Q4 typically softer

signals.signal_summary: Instamart contribution margin improved to -1.8% of GOV (improved 65 bps QoQ); monthly CM reached -1.1% by March 2026. Adj. EBITDA loss Rs 858 crore.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Log CM trajectory; model breakeven based on -1.1% March run-rate
signals.limitation_or_caveat: CM still negative; EBITDA losses remain very large at Rs 858 crore/quarter

signals.signal_summary: Instamart operates 1,143 dark stores across 129 cities, covering 4.8 million sq ft. Added ~109 stores during Q4 FY26.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update store count; compare with Blinkit 2,243 and Zepto 1,000+
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Store count includes newly launched stores

signals.signal_summary: Instamart AOV Rs 700, up 32.8% YoY. Platform MTUs 25.2 million (up 27% YoY). Food delivery MTUs 18.3 million (up 21% YoY).
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update AOV comparison (Instamart Rs 700 vs Blinkit Rs 525 vs Zepto Rs 470-550)
signals.limitation_or_caveat: AOV uplift driven by category mix shift to non-grocery; may not be sustainable

signals.signal_summary: Swiggy management targeting Instamart contribution margin breakeven by Q1 FY27 (Apr-Jun 2026). CM improved 65 bps QoQ.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add to breakeven timeline tracker; compare with Blinkit's achieved EBITDA+
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Guidance not achieved; competitive discounting could delay

signals.signal_summary: Swiggy consolidated FY26 net loss Rs 4,154 crore (widened 33% YoY); Q4 loss narrowed to Rs 800 crore from Rs 1,081 crore YoY. Ad/promotional spend surged 55% YoY to Rs 4,207 crore in FY26.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Compute implied CAC per order; flag as CAC pressure indicator
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Covers both food delivery and quick commerce; cannot isolate Instamart-specific spend

signals.signal_summary: Swiggy FY26 revenue Rs 23,052 crore (up 51% YoY); raised Rs 10,000 crore via QIP in Dec 2025. Market cap Rs 78,062 crore.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update Swiggy P&L panel; note QIP fundraise
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Consolidated figure includes food delivery; Instamart standalone not fully separated

signals.signal_summary: Instamart saw 49x surge in gold orders on Akshaya Tritiya 2026 vs normal day, driven by small-ticket gold items.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add festival-driven category spikes as campaign trigger insight
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Single-day event spike; not representative of steady-state demand

signals.signal_summary: Instamart FY25 adjusted EBITDA loss Rs 2,095 crore, significantly wider than Blinkit's Rs 292 crore adjusted EBITDA loss.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add to FY25 comparative P&L panel
signals.limitation_or_caveat: EBITDA loss not directly comparable to Zepto's net loss figure

signals.signal_summary: Zepto raised $450M in a pre-IPO round at a $7B valuation, up 40% from $5B in its prior round. CEO stated the company has ~$900M net cash.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update valuation timeline $5B to $7B; flag pre-IPO status
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Valuation from secondary reporting; company has not publicly filed updated cap table

signals.signal_summary: Zepto shareholders approved raising up to Rs 11,000 crore via fresh equity issue. SEBI in-principle approval received. IPO expected Jul-Sep 2026.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add Zepto to IPO watchlist with estimated listing window Jul-Sep 2026
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Confidential filing; SEBI in-principle approval reported but not independently verified

signals.signal_summary: Zepto FY25 turnover Rs 11,110 crore (~150% YoY). However, audited financials show total sales Rs 9,669 crore (129% growth) and operational revenue estimated at only Rs 1,495-1,994 crore.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Record both turnover and operational revenue separately; flag accounting distinction
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Turnover includes non-operational income; operational revenue est. only Rs 1,495-1,994 Crore; not directly comparable to Blinkit/Instamart GOV

signals.signal_summary: Zepto FY25 net loss widened 177% YoY to Rs 3,367 crore. Losses rose to ~35% of turnover, up from 29% in FY24.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update loss trajectory; note deterioration in loss/revenue ratio 29% to 35%
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Loss figure includes ESOP costs and expansion expenses; comparability affected by accounting changes

signals.signal_summary: CEO announced Zepto nearing $4 billion in annualised gross order value, doubling from $3B in Dec 2024. EBITDA (ex-ESOPs) and OCF burn halved over 3 months.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Plot GOV trajectory; flag as company-reported vs audited
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Annualised GOV is a run-rate metric, not an audited fiscal-year figure

signals.signal_summary: Zepto processes 1.7M+ daily orders and holds ~29% market share. Adding ~5.9M new customers per quarter.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Record daily orders; cross-validate with Blinkit ~2.6M/day
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Company-claimed figures; market share estimate from secondary aggregation

signals.signal_summary: Zepto reported first group-level EBITDA-positive month in late 2025. 60%+ of mature dark stores are EBITDA-positive. Monthly burn reduced to ~Rs 100 Cr.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Log monthly burn rate and store EBITDA milestone; note FY29 profitability target
signals.limitation_or_caveat: EBITDA-positive month does not indicate sustained quarterly profitability; net loss continues to widen

signals.signal_summary: Zepto launched Jarvis in-house ad platform. Crossed Rs 1,000 crore in annualised advertising revenue (~Rs 83 Cr/month).
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Track Zepto ad revenue as % of total revenue; benchmark vs Blinkit ad revenue
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Annualised figure extrapolated from monthly run rate; not audited

signals.signal_summary: Zepto partnered with The Trade Desk for open-internet retail media. 60M+ monthly active users targeted across 80+ cities with closed-loop measurement. Covers CTV, OTT, music streaming.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Track MAU figure and partnership ecosystem; flag data-sharing implications
signals.limitation_or_caveat: MAU figure is company-reported; partnership scope beyond India not clarified

signals.signal_summary: Zepto deepened retail media partnership with Criteo for off-site advertising, enabling re-engagement of Zepto shoppers across the open internet.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Log Criteo + Trade Desk partnerships as retail media infrastructure signals
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Financial terms of partnership not disclosed

signals.signal_summary: Zepto removed ~300 employees from payroll, transferring them to third-party service providers. ~1,000 employees impacted YTD 2025 across multiple rounds. CEO emphasised cost obsession.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Track headcount reduction and automation investment; correlate with burn rate claims
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Some employees were reassigned rather than terminated

signals.signal_summary: BigB basket consolidated net loss widened 42% YoY to Rs 2,006.8 crore in FY25, while operating revenue dipped 2% to Rs 9,866.7 crore.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Flag negative trajectory; track quarterly burn vs dark-store contribution margin
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Consolidated figure includes B2B arm; B2C-specific loss is worse

signals.signal_summary: BigB basket B2C unit (Innovative Retail Concepts) turnover declined 3% to Rs 7,673 Cr while loss surged 47% to Rs 1,851 Cr.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Alert on negative unit economics; annotate with Blinkit/Zepto benchmarks
signals.limitation_or_caveat: B2C unit includes legacy slotted + BB Now; pure BB Now economics not separately disclosed

signals.signal_summary: BigB basket operates ~700 dark stores across Tier-I and Tier-II cities, planning 900 by end-2025. Top 25% of stores already profitable. Target 1,000-1,200 stores for food delivery rollout.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Track rollout vs target; model contribution margin by store cohort
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Top 25% profitability claim is selective; overall business remains deeply loss-making

signals.signal_summary: BigB basket marketing spend surged 51% YoY to Rs 497 Cr in FY25. Private labels (Fresho, BB Royal) contributed ~30% of sales.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Track marketing spend as % of revenue; monitor CAC efficiency
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Marketing spend includes both BB Now and legacy BigBasket brand spend

signals.signal_summary: CEO stated BigB basket targeting IPO in ~2 years, aiming to double business by March 2026 before going public. Tata raising $1.3B external capital, with $1B earmarked for BigBasket.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Track quarterly revenue/loss trajectory; IPO timeline may slip if FY26 losses do not narrow
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Forward-looking; subject to market conditions and profitability progress

signals.signal_summary: Dunzo's app and website went offline on Jan 13, 2025, after cofounder/CEO Kabeer Biswas departed to join Flipkart Minutes. Marks formal end of Dunzo's business after 18 months of minimal operations.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Remove Dunzo from active competitor watchlist; redistribute market share estimates; add as cautionary case study
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Shutdown was widely anticipated; market had already priced this in

signals.signal_summary: Reliance Retail wrote off entire Rs 1,645 Crore investment in Dunzo. Dunzo generated just Rs 1 Cr in operating revenue in FY25, down from Rs 226 Cr in FY23.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Flag as cautionary QC investment thesis; use loss ratio as warning benchmark
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Write-off is accounting; Reliance may still hold claims in NCLT proceedings

signals.signal_summary: Dunzo FY23 losses surged ~4X YoY to Rs 1,801 Cr on revenue of just Rs 226 Cr — a loss-to-revenue ratio of ~8:1. Last full fiscal year before shutdown.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Use as benchmark case study for unsustainable QC burn (8:1 loss:revenue ratio)
signals.limitation_or_caveat: FY23 was peak expansion year; FY24/25 data is partial/shutdown-mode

signals.signal_summary: Google and Deloitte estimate India QC GMV at $7-8B in 2025, projected to grow ~6x to $45-50B by 2030. User base projected to nearly double from 32-34M to 65-70M by 2030. Non-food categories to reach 40-45% of market. ~80% of QC GMV currently from larger cities; Tier II+ expected to reach ~30% by 2030.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update market sizing panel with $7-8B baseline and $45-50B 2030 projection. Add Tier-II trajectory.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Projection to 2030 is model-based; depends on regulatory clarity, unit economics, and competitive intensity

signals.signal_summary: Competitive dark store counts as of Dec 2025: Blinkit ~2,027; Zepto ~1,100; Instamart ~1,034. Blinkit daily orders ~2.6M/day; Instamart ~1.2M/day.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update dark store count and daily orders comparison table
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Figures as of Dec 2025; store counts change rapidly

signals.signal_summary: India has ~2,525 operational dark stores across 8 Tier-I cities and 100+ Tier-II/III cities, occupying ~13M sq ft. Projected to grow 3x to ~7,500 by 2030. Tier-I cities hold 68% of stores.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update dark store count to 2,525; add Tier-I vs Tier-II/III breakdown (68%/32%)
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Savills figure may not capture all smaller/newer operators; 2030 projection assumes current growth trajectory

signals.signal_summary: Bernstein projects 75%+ YoY growth in India's QC sector for 2025, significantly outpacing traditional retail. QC expanding beyond groceries into new categories and Tier-2+ cities. Top 40-50 cities constitute a $250 billion grocery market.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add 75%+ growth estimate; track category diversification beyond grocery; log $250B addressable market
signals.limitation_or_caveat: 75% is an industry estimate; growth rates may slow as base increases

signals.signal_summary: Significant regulatory uncertainty over whether dark stores qualify as marketplace platforms (FDI permitted) or inventory-based retailers (FDI prohibited). DPIIT may issue clarifications. QC and dark store not defined in FDI policy. On-ground inspections for food safety compliance underway.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add regulatory risk alert module; track DPIPT policy announcements; flag FDI classification risk
signals.limitation_or_caveat: No regulatory action taken yet — this is a risk signal, not an event

signals.signal_summary: Amazon India investing Rs 2,000 crore in QC infrastructure, targeting 300+ dark stores. Flipkart crossed 800 stores, targeting ~1,600 by end-2026.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add Amazon Now and Flipkart Minutes to competition dashboard; track store count targets vs actuals
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Target store counts may include stores in various stages of readiness, not all fully operational

signals.signal_summary: Quick-commerce platforms collectively expected to clock Rs 4,900 crore in ad revenue in 2026, up ~63% from Rs 3,000 crore YoY.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Build Q-Commerce Ad Revenue trend card; flag 63% YoY growth as high-priority insight
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Rs 4,900 Cr projection by Datum Intelligence; may include some e-commerce overlap; platform-level breakdowns not publicly disclosed

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit and Zepto each crossed Rs 1,000 crore in annual advertising revenue by FY25. Blinkit's ad revenue grew ~220% YoY, now constituting ~15% of total revenue.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add Blinkit vs Zepto vs Instamart ad revenue comparison; flag ad-revenue-as-%-of-total-revenue as key KPI
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Exact Instamart ad revenue not individually disclosed; Zepto figure from founder statement, not formal filing

signals.signal_summary: Q-commerce ad ROAS is 1.5-2x higher than Meta/Google, with conversion rates of 3-8%. Brands are tripling QC ad budgets.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Build Q-Commerce vs Traditional Digital ROAS comparator; add conversion rate range slider (3-8%)
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Ranges based on aggregated brand reports, not independent audit; performance varies by category, city tier, and campaign quality

signals.signal_summary: India remained WhatsApp's largest market with 853M+ active users in 2025. WhatsApp paid messaging crossed $2B in annualized revenue by Q4 2025. 175M people message a WhatsApp Business account daily. 2.2B business-customer messages exchanged daily.
signals.metric_name: WhatsApp India MAU; Paid Messaging Revenue
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Build WhatsApp India user tracker (853M+); add WhatsApp Business messaging revenue growth chart
signals.limitation_or_caveat: $2B is global, not India-specific; India revenue share not disclosed by Meta

signals.signal_summary: WhatsApp Business messages achieve 95-98% open rates vs 20-25% for email. CTR 15-60%. Average response time 45-90 seconds. 90% of messages read within 3 minutes.
signals.metric_name: WhatsApp Open Rate; CTR; Response Time
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Build WhatsApp vs Email vs SMS comparison module; highlight WhatsApp as highest-engagement commerce channel
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Open rates vary by message type (transactional vs promotional), industry, and opt-in quality; CTR range is wide

signals.signal_summary: Meta's click-to-WhatsApp ad revenue grew 60% YoY in Q3 2025. Family of Apps other revenue hit $690M (up 59%). India powers Meta's $1B+ paid messaging business. New India-specific WhatsApp Business features launched: in-app payments, voice calling for enterprises, ads in WhatsApp Status.
signals.metric_name: Click-to-WhatsApp Ad Revenue Growth; FoA Other Revenue
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Track click-to-WhatsApp ad revenue growth quarterly; add WhatsApp Feature Rollout timeline card for India
signals.limitation_or_caveat: $690M is total FoA other revenue (includes Meta Verified, not just WhatsApp); India-specific revenue not broken out

signals.signal_summary: 57% of India's internet users prefer vernacular content. 88% trust local-language content more than English. 90% of new internet users prefer regional languages. 45% make purchases based on videos from creators in their native tongue.
signals.metric_name: Vernacular Content Preference; Trust; Purchase Influence
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Build Vernacular Readiness Index: 57% preference, 88% trust, 45% purchase influence; flag language gap alerts
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Survey data may reflect social desirability bias; overlap between preference and purchase behavior not well-established

signals.signal_summary: Vernacular content delivers 1.5-2x higher engagement vs English-first content. 70% of Indians find vernacular digital content more reliable than English. Regional CTR significantly higher than English equivalents.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Build Vernacular vs English engagement comparator; enable language-wise A/B testing recommendations
signals.limitation_or_caveat: 1.5-2x is aggregated; actual lift varies by language, category, and creative quality

signals.signal_summary: AOV comparison: Blinkit Rs 525 (net), Swiggy Instamart Rs 700 (gross), Zepto Rs 470-550. Instamart briefly overtook Blinkit AOV in Q2 FY26.
signals.metric_value: Blinkit Rs 525; Instamart Rs 700; Zepto Rs 470-550
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Build comparative AOV time-series chart; track convergence/divergence trends
signals.limitation_or_caveat: AOV definitions may differ (gross vs net); Blinkit Rs 525 is net AOV, Instamart Rs 700 appears to be gross

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit CAC estimated at Rs 1,240 with CLV of Rs 890 and 47.35% churn rate — CAC exceeds CLV, indicating negative unit economics per customer.
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add CAC/CLV panel with confidence caveat; cross-reference against Swiggy ad spend as proxy
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Third-party estimate, not company-disclosed; CAC likely varies by city tier and cohort

signals.signal_summary: India QC market crossed Rs 64,000 crore (~$7.7B). AOV ~Rs 546 in hyperlocal delivery. Average monthly orders per QC customer rose from 4.4 (2021) to 6 (2024). Dark store breakeven in Tier 2 at ~800 orders/day vs ~1,300 in metros. Last-mile delivery comprises 40-55% of total logistics cost.
signals.metric_name: QC Market Size; AOV; Frequency; Breakeven
signals.metric_value: Rs 64,000 crore market; Rs 546 AOV; 6 orders/customer/month; 800 orders/day breakeven (Tier 2)
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Build frequency + AOV trend dashboard; add Tier-2 breakeven calculator (800 vs 1,300 orders/day)
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Rs 64,000 crore may include broader hyperlocal delivery; AOV and frequency data aggregated from multiple sources with different methodologies

platformIntelligence.name: Blinkit
platformIntelligence.currentRole: Market leader — first QC platform to achieve EBITDA profitability
publicMetrics: Q4 FY26 NOV: Rs 14,386 Cr (95.4% YoY)
Q4 FY26 Adj. EBITDA: Rs 37 Cr (0.3% of NOV)
Ad revenue: >Rs 1,000 Cr annualised (Rs 400 Cr quarterly)
risks: AOV stagnation suggests limited pricing power
Revenue comparison distorted by inventory model shift
platformIntelligence.gtmImplication: Use as profitability benchmark; track EBITDA margin trajectory toward 5-6% target

platformIntelligence.name: Swiggy Instamart
platformIntelligence.currentRole: Scale player with highest AOV, targeting CM breakeven Jun 2026
publicMetrics: Q4 FY26 GOV: Rs 7,881 Cr (68.8% YoY)
Adj. EBITDA loss: Rs 858 Cr Q4 FY26
risks: EBITDA losses remain large at Rs 858 Cr/quarter
Ad/promotional spend surged 55% to Rs 4,207 Cr FY26
platformIntelligence.gtmImplication: High-AOV benchmark for premium category campaigns; watch CM trajectory for breakeven signal

platformIntelligence.name: Zepto
platformIntelligence.currentRole: High-growth challenger, IPO candidate, aggressive retail media player
publicMetrics: FY25 net loss: Rs 3,367 Cr (177% wider)
Jarvis ad platform: Rs 1,000 Cr annualised
risks: IPO while unprofitable carries market risk
Revenue accounting differs from rivals (gross vs net)
platformIntelligence.gtmImplication: Fastest growth but highest risk; retail media is key differentiator; Criteo/Trade Desk partnerships show ad maturity

platformIntelligence.name: BigB basket Now
platformIntelligence.currentRole: Retail-backed grocery platform, deep losses, scale risk
publicMetrics: FY25 consolidated loss: Rs 2,006.8 Cr (+42% YoY)
risks: No public QC-specific GMV or market share
platformIntelligence.gtmImplication: Qualitative grocery benchmark only; do not use as positive benchmark until GMV disclosed

platformIntelligence.name: Dunzo — Historical Failure Case
platformIntelligence.currentRole: Shut down Jan 2025 — cautionary benchmark only
publicMetrics: Rs 1,645 Cr Reliance investment written off
FY23 net loss: Rs 1,801 Cr on Rs 226 Cr revenue (8:1 ratio)
risks: Operational failure — not an active competitor
platformIntelligence.gtmImplication: Use exclusively as a cautionary case study: over-ambitious QC pivot + unsustainable burn = existential failure
